formatExamples.Paragraph:
This is a standard paragraph format. Text flows naturally with proper spacing and readability. You can include multiple paragraphs to explain concepts, provide background information, or tell a story.

Each paragraph builds on the previous one, creating a cohesive narrative that guides the reader through your content.

blog-info.label: Blog / Info
blog-info.overview: Blog and informational content targets users at the top of the funnel who recognize they have a problem but haven't yet identified a specific solution. This content educates, informs, and builds awareness around industry trends, challenges, and foundational concepts. It establishes thought leadership and organic search visibility for broad, high-volume informational queries. Success is measured by engagement, time-on-page, and follow-up conversions to more specific solution-oriented content.
bestPractices: Use clear, descriptive headlines that answer common questions
Include data, statistics, or case studies to build credibility
Break content into scannable sections with subheadings
Link to related guides and solution pages for progressive engagement
Optimize for long-tail keywords and question-based queries
Include visual elements (charts, infographics) to enhance comprehension
blog-info.key_topics: You can copy in contents from your existing pages or use AI suggest outline to get started.

guide.label: Guide
guide.overview: Guides are in-depth, solution-focused resources for users who've identified a specific solution category and want to understand how it works, best practices for implementation, and comparative evaluation criteria. These pages rank for mid-funnel keywords and serve as decision-support content that moves prospects closer to a purchasing decision. Guides build authority by providing comprehensive, actionable information that demonstrates deep expertise in a specific domain.
bestPractices: Structure content with clear, logical progression from basics to advanced
Include step-by-step instructions or checklists
Provide comparison frameworks or evaluation criteria
Use tables or matrices to simplify complex information
Include implementation examples or code snippets where relevant
Link to related product/service pages and deeper resources
Anticipate and answer objections to the solution
guide.key_topics: You can copy in contents from your existing pages or use AI suggest outline to get started.

service-page.label: Service Page
service-page.overview: Service pages describe the specific services your organization offers, targeting prospects in the consideration stage who have identified their problem and solution category but are evaluating providers. These pages must clearly communicate service scope, unique value propositions, methodologies, outcomes, and differentiators. They balance persuasive copywriting with credibility signals and typically include client testimonials, case studies, and clear calls-to-action that drive qualified leads.
bestPractices: Lead with the primary benefit and pain point addressed
Clearly define service scope and what's included
Use social proof: testimonials, case studies, client logos
Explain your unique methodology or approach
Quantify results and outcomes with metrics
Include clear pricing or inquiry CTAs
Address common objections and FAQs
Include video testimonials or demonstrations
service-page.key_topics: You can copy in contents from your existing pages or use AI suggest outline to get started.

product-page.label: Product Page
product-page.overview: Product pages showcase a specific product offering with detailed feature descriptions, benefits, technical specifications, and value propositions. These pages target prospects actively evaluating your solution and must clearly communicate differentiation, use cases, and integration capabilities. They balance technical depth with accessibility, include visual demonstrations, pricing information, and strong conversion-focused CTAs that drive trial signups, demos, or purchases.
bestPractices: Lead with primary use case and target customer
Use clear product imagery and demo videos
List key features with benefit explanations
Include technical specifications and requirements
Show integration capabilities
Display pricing clearly (if applicable)
Include customer logos and testimonials
Provide links to documentation and support
A/B test CTAs and messaging
product-page.key_topics: You can copy in contents from your existing pages or use AI suggest outline to get started.

pillar-page.label: Pillar Page
pillar-page.overview: Pillar pages are comprehensive, authority-building resources that cover a broad topic in significant depth while linking to more specific, narrower "cluster" content. These pages target high-value, competitive keywords and establish topical authority for search engines. Pillar pages serve as hubs that connect related content and provide SEO benefits through a cluster topology. They typically rank for primary, high-volume keywords and drive traffic to more specific, conversion-focused cluster pages.
bestPractices: Cover the topic exhaustively but remain readable
Use clear table of contents with internal anchor links
Link to related cluster content strategically
Use consistent terminology and taxonomy
Include visual elements for complex concepts
Update regularly with new insights and statistics
Maintain 5,000+ words for topical authority
Use schema markup for enhanced search visibility
Include expert quotes and citations
pillar-page.key_topics: You can copy in contents from your existing pages or use AI suggest outline to get started.

homepage.label: Homepage
homepage.overview: The homepage is the digital front door of your organization, serving both new visitors discovering your brand and returning users seeking specific information. It must clearly communicate your value proposition, build credibility, and guide users to relevant content or conversion actions. The homepage balances brand messaging with functional navigation, typically including sections on products/services, customer success, and clear calls-to-action. It's optimized for both brand searches and for establishing first impressions.
bestPractices: Lead with clear, concise value proposition
Use compelling hero image or video
Highlight key products/services prominently
Include social proof: logos, testimonials, metrics
Make navigation intuitive and clear
Feature customer success stories or case studies
Include multiple CTAs at key points
Mobile-first responsive design
Fast loading times and performance
Clear secondary messaging about unique selling points
homepage.key_topics: You can copy in contents from your existing pages or use AI suggest outline to get started.
